message:
fix: surface 401 as expired-token error on feedback submit (#8864)

When the FEEDBACK_GITHUB_TOKEN PAT is invalid or expired, GitHub rejects
the issue-creation POST with a 401. The handler was wrapping every
postGitHubIssue error in a generic 502 Bad Gateway — "Failed to create
GitHub issue: ...", which the frontend displays alongside static
"install a GitHub OAuth app" guidance. Users whose real problem is a
stale PAT end up hunting for nonexistent OAuth-app configuration.

Adds an `errGitHubUnauthorized` sentinel returned from postGitHubIssue
when GitHub responds 401, and branches in createFeatureRequest so that
case maps to HTTP 401 with message: "FEEDBACK_GITHUB_TOKEN is invalid
or expired. Refresh the PAT in your .env and restart the console."
Other upstream failures keep the existing 502 path.

Addresses the bead scanner-beads-t2c (local-dev reporter hit this with
an expired PAT on the Contribute flow; the generic OAuth-app guidance
sent them on a wild goose chase).

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
